agent_scopes.mechanism: scope from file location: root-level = workspace-wide always-on, subdirectory = subtree glob; git root discovery extends scope to parent directories within the repo

definition_format.mechanism: AGENTS.md (or agents.md) plain markdown files with no frontmatter; scope inferred from directory location (root = always-on, subdirectory = auto-glob subtree)

invocation_patterns.mechanism: AGENTS.md files are always-on rule injection, not invocable agents; no manual invocation or delegation mechanism

model_selection.mechanism: AGENTS.md has no frontmatter; no per-agent model selection

per_agent_mcp.mechanism: AGENTS.md has no structured config; MCP is workspace-wide only, not per-agent

subagent_spawning.mechanism: no structured agent orchestration; AGENTS.md is rules injection piggybacking on the Rules engine, not an agent harness

tool_restrictions.mechanism: AGENTS.md contains free-form instructions only; no structured tool restriction mechanism — it is a rules delivery format, not an agent harness

providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md: plain markdown with location-inferred scope
providerExtensions.description: Windsurf's agent instruction format is AGENTS.md (or agents.md — case insensitive), a plain markdown file with no required frontmatter. Scope is inferred from file location: root-level files apply to all files in the workspace (always-on); any subdirectory AGENTS.md applies to files within that subtree (auto-glob `<dir>/**`). Multiple AGENTS.md files can coexist at different directory levels for increasingly specific guidance. Both filenames are recognized.

providerExtensions.name: Discovery up to git root across parent directories
providerExtensions.description: For git repositories, Windsurf searches parent directories up to the git root when discovering AGENTS.md files. This means AGENTS.md files in parent directories above the workspace root (but within the same git repo) are also picked up. Multiple open workspace folders are deduplicated.
